questionText: Decide which claim about this teddy-bear token game is true. A fair bag has 9 tokens: 2 blue bears, 3 green bears, and 4 pink bears. One token is picked and put back each time. After 45 picks, blue bear appeared 13 times. Choose the best judgment about comparing the experimental probability with the theoretical probability for blue bear.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can compare theoretical and experimental probability
issues: [difficulty_mismatch, distractor_quality] | The item is labeled hard, but solving it only requires a straightforward comparison of 13/45 with 2/9 or noting that 2/9 of 45 is 10, so the cognitive demand is closer to medium. | Several distractors are weak and implausible rather than reflecting realistic student misconceptions, such as comparing odd numbers or saying 13 is close to 9. | Fix: Relabel the item as medium, or increase complexity by requiring evaluation of a more nuanced claim, justification with reasoning, or analysis of a common error. | Fix: Replace weak distractors with misconception-based options, such as comparing 13 to 10 incorrectly, confusing 2/9 with 2/45, or claiming 13/45 is less because 13 is less than 45.
